emails.subject: Your Ultimate Costa Rica Packing Guide (+ Free Checklist!)
emails.content:
COSTA RICA PACKING GUIDE

Headed to Costa Rica? Here's everything you need:

CLOTHING
- Lightweight moisture-wicking layers
- Swimsuits (at least 2)
- Quick-dry shorts and tank tops
- One nice outfit for dinner
- Rain jacket or packable poncho
- Hiking shoes + flip flops

GEAR
- Reef-safe sunscreen SPF 50+
- Insect repellent (DEET or picaridin)
- Dry bag for water activities
- Binoculars for wildlife
- Portable charger

DOCUMENTS
- Passport (valid 6+ months past travel)
- Travel insurance docs
- Printed backup of confirmations

Download our free printable checklist: thepackinglist.co/costa-rica

Happy travels!
The Packing List Team

emails.subject: Managing Blood Sugar During Outdoor Activity
emails.content:
read later

https://www.webmd.com/diabetes/blood-sugar-outdoor-activity

Managing Blood Sugar During Outdoor Activity

Spending time outdoors is great exercise, but if you have prediabetes or diabetes, planning ahead helps.

- Eat a balanced meal 1-2 hours before activity
- Bring snacks with complex carbs like trail mix or whole grain crackers
- Stay hydrated with water, not sugary sports drinks
- Monitor how you feel and take breaks as needed
- Avoid exercising in extreme heat
- For long outdoor activities like hiking or fishing, pack more food than you think you need

emails.subject: V3.0 API Spec - Scope Changes
emails.content:
Destiny,

Updated v3.0 spec is attached. Main changes from rc1:

- Cursor-based pagination replacing offset pagination on all list endpoints
- Two new endpoints: /v3/audit-logs and /v3/account/usage-metrics
- Webhook payload schema updated with new event_metadata field
- Breaking change: /v3/rate-limits response is nested objects now instead of flat

Felicia said docs deadline is Feb 28 so flagging early. Let me know if you have questions.

Raj

emails.subject: Re: V3.0 API Spec - Scope Changes
emails.content:
Thanks Raj. Quick questions:

1. Cursor-based pagination - is the cursor opaque or base64-encoded? All the offset examples need rewriting.
2. New endpoints - same auth model? Bearer token, same scopes?
3. Webhook change - backward compatible or breaking for existing integrations?

Trying to scope how much rework this is.

emails.subject: Re: V3.0 API Spec - Scope Changes
emails.content:
1. Opaque string. Don't document the internals, just show the cursor field in the response and next_cursor param.
2. Same auth, same scopes.
3. Breaking. Existing webhooks get the new payload format in v3.0. Worth calling that out in migration docs.

Spec should be stable now.

emails.subject: V3.0 Docs - Deadline
emails.content:
Feb 28 deadline. No extensions.

- Product launch is March 10
- Docs go into the release package
- Scheduling a spec walkthrough for Saturday

Calendar invite incoming.

emails.subject: Re: V3.0 Docs - Deadline
emails.content: Got it. Saturday works.

emails.subject: Re: V3.0 API Spec - Scope Changes
emails.content: Quick correction - the usage metrics endpoint path is /v3/account/usage-metrics, not /v3/usage-metrics. Updated spec attached.

emails.subject: V3.0 Draft Posted
emails.content:
First draft is up on the docs portal. Link in the docs-team channel.

Covers auth, webhooks, rate limiting, pagination, and the new endpoints. Error codes still in progress.

emails.subject: Re: V3.0 Draft Posted
emails.content:
Left comments on the draft. Two sections need rework:

- Pagination examples: still offset-based. Need cursor-based examples.
- Webhook payload schema: outdated. Use the v3.0 structure from the spec.

Pagination is the priority. Deadline is Friday.

emails.subject: Rate-limit doc draft - retry semantics question
emails.content:
Hey D - read your rate-limit draft this morning. Two questions on retry semantics:
1. For 429 responses, do you want clients to back off using the Retry-After header value or our exponential schedule?
2. Bucket exhaustion vs sustained-rate exhaustion - the spec treats them the same right now. Should the docs spell that out?
Happy to jump on a 15-min call if easier. -R

emails.subject: Re: Rate-limit doc draft - retry semantics question
emails.content:
Both good catches.
1. Retry-After takes precedence; our schedule is the fallback when the header is absent. I'll add a note + a code sample.
2. Agreed - I'll split the section. Bucket exhaustion gets its own subsection because it's the more common case people hit.
Aiming to push v2 of the doc by EOD Thursday. Will you be reviewing live or async?

emails.subject: Style guide v2 - your section on voice & tone
emails.content:
Destiny - took a pass on your voice & tone section yesterday. Couple of notes:
- Love the "explain like the reader hasn't slept" framing. Keeping it.
- The "avoid em-dashes in body copy" rule conflicts with what we agreed last quarter. Soften to a guideline?
- Add 2-3 before/after examples for the "prefer second person" rule. That one always trips up the new hires.
Otherwise solid. Want this to land before the v3.0 launch so the API ref matches the new tone.

emails.subject: Re: Style guide v2 - your section on voice & tone
emails.content:
Got it - I'll soften the em-dash rule to a guideline and add the second-person examples. Probably pulling from the auth and webhook docs as the before/after sources since those went through the heaviest revision.
EOD Thursday for v2. Anything else you want to land before the v3.0 launch?
